mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market fundamentals, which reduces expected consumer demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns as growth expectations are revised downwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment was 8.9% in April 2009 and rising, a key focus for markets during the GFC recovery.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank Information Coefficient between UNRATE monthly changes and S&P 500 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month rebound in retail sales following a period of contraction indicates a bottoming of consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in corporate revenue forecasts and positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 1-month % change > 1% after 3 consecutive months of negative change.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales contracted sharply in late 2008, making any sign of a rebound a powerful potential signal in early 2009.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is near the zero lower bound, an acceleration in inflation compresses real yields, boosting the present value of future corporate earnings and encouraging flows into risk assets like equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve initiated its Zero Interest-Rate Policy (ZIRP) in December 2008.
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals a recovery in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, improving corporate profitability outlooks and driving positive equity returns, especially after a recession.
evidence_refs: doc:NBER recession dating shows the GFC trough was in June 2009, making recovery signals critical.
failure_condition: The signal has a hit rate below 55% for positive 60-day returns during periods officially dated as economic recovery by the NBER.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected decline in nonfarm payrolls below a critical threshold signals a severe contraction in economic activity, leading to immediate downward revisions of earnings forecasts and negative short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS prints were deeply negative throughout late 2008 and early 2009, exceeding -700k multiple times.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts, a key leading indicator, signals a recovery in household formation and investment, but only generates positive equity returns when broad economic output is no longer contracting.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market collapse was central to the GFC, making its recovery a key focus for investors.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for positive 60-day returns is not statistically different when GDPC1 q/q change is positive versus when it is negative.

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate serves as a key component of the discount rate for equity valuation. A higher rate increases the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future earnings, exerting downward pressure on stock prices.
evidence_refs: doc:Standard discounted cash flow (DCF) models in finance textbooks.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high unemployment rate and contracting retail sales indicates a severe and broad-based weakness in consumer health, leading to sharp downward revisions in revenue expectations and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:In early 2009, UNRATE was rising sharply past 8% while RSAFS was deeply negative year-over-year.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6%, the correlation between RSAFS momentum and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is non-negative over a 5-year rolling window.
